purpose: User-facing capability register. This is not the internal actor capability registry; it exists so release value starts from what a user can do.

capabilities.name: Traceability
capabilities.description: AOF can link work, requirements, sessions, decisions, evidence, and release readiness into an auditable chain.
capabilities.user_value: Users can reconstruct why work exists and what evidence supports it without reading every raw artifact.

capabilities.name: Organization Memory
capabilities.description: AOF can keep canonical state for tasks, goals, runtime sessions, decisions, artifacts, and learning updates.
capabilities.user_value: Users can continue governed work across sessions instead of relying on one long chat transcript.

capabilities.name: Governance
capabilities.description: AOF can separate maker work, checker review, council judgment, and release gates.
capabilities.user_value: Users can stop work from being called done only because the maker says it is done.

capabilities.name: Human Approval
capabilities.description: AOF can record independent human approval and revocation state for external-write candidates.
capabilities.user_value: Users can distinguish Council review from explicit human permission before external side effects.

capabilities.name: External Resource Access
capabilities.description: AOF can represent external actors, tools, providers, and references with source-of-truth and permission boundaries.
capabilities.user_value: Users can see what external resource is being used, for what purpose, and under what limits.

capabilities.name: Provider Execution
capabilities.description: AOF can model provider execution readiness, approval, reproduction, rollback, outcome, learning, production-boundary candidates, and controlled execution candidates.
capabilities.user_value: Users can see when a provider-backed action is only a controlled candidate and cannot be confused with production execution.

capabilities.name: Provider Incident Recovery
capabilities.description: AOF can record and audit a provider incident drill that links a controlled execution candidate to detection, containment, rollback, recovery, notification, learning, and resume/stop governance.
capabilities.user_value: Users can inspect what happens if a provider-backed action fails before allowing the action to advance.

capabilities.name: Rollback
capabilities.description: AOF can require rollback readiness evidence before provider execution claims are trusted.
capabilities.user_value: Users can avoid treating an external operation as acceptable when reversal evidence is missing.

capabilities.name: Learning
capabilities.description: AOF can convert outcome evidence into an accepted, corrected, rollback, escalation, or defer learning update.
capabilities.user_value: Users can make the next decision depend on what happened, rather than losing feedback after release.

capabilities.name: Product Value Comprehension
capabilities.description: AOF can require release value evidence that states what a user can now do, before/after, demo, reduced work, and not-proven boundaries.
capabilities.user_value: Users do not need to infer release value from records, audits, schemas, or dashboard existence.

capabilities.name: Capability-First Release
capabilities.description: AOF can require a formal capability register, version matrix, capability delta, and release acceptance criteria before release-ready claims.
capabilities.user_value: Users can understand what changed in a release within 30 seconds before reading implementation details.
